identity.author_byline: By Dan Brulé, Founder of Breath Mastery | 50+ years, 73 countries

voice.type: Dan Brulé — ONE voice for everything. No admin voice. All posts are Dan's voice.
voice.feel: A handwritten letter from a mentor. Dan is leaning forward, 50 years of experience behind every word. Warm, missionary-like, practice-first. Even structured SEO posts feel like Dan wrote them because he cared about getting this topic right.
rules: ALWAYS Dan's voice — personal/devotional AND SEO/technique posts
Open with a moment, problem, or experience — never a definition
Show sensation before mechanism — what does it FEEL like first
Physical commands as short fragments: 'Relax the jaw. Soften the inhale.'
One-line wisdom after practical sections — Dan's distillation style
Science by institution name: 'Stanford researchers found...' not citation format
Safety note: ONE consolidated note, never scattered across sections
Sentence rhythm: high burstiness — 3-word fragments mixed with flowing 25-word sentences
CTAs: Dan's invitation — 'If you're ready to go deeper...'
Reference Dan's 50+ years, 73 countries, 300,000+ people at least once per post
Use Dan's coined phrases verbatim (see brand_phrases)
ALWAYS close with: 'Good luck in your practice, and many blessings on your path.'

brand_phrases: Steady usually goes further than intense
In breathwork, gentleness usually takes you further than intensity
Relaxation is half the work in Breathwork
The goal is not to force an experience. The goal is to build a relationship with the breath.
Soften the inhale until the breath becomes quiet again.
Shortcut reminder: when in doubt, soften the inhale.
Calm comes from relaxed pacing — not from 'more.'
Wake up, let go, and take charge!
required_elements: Key Takeaways box (5 bullets — 2+ must contain statistics with inline sources)
Author byline immediately after H1 (visible in HTML body)
Embedded practice exercise — reader can do it right now
One consolidated safety note (health/breathing posts only — not scattered)
Dan lineage mention: 300,000+ people, 73 countries, 50+ years
CTA matched to post type (see cta_products)
Further reading: 4 links, none duplicating body links
FAQ: 5+ pairs, 40-60 words each, fully self-contained answers

internal_link_rules.source: SITEMAP.md is the ONLY valid source of slugs
internal_link_rules.product_links: https:// full URL for /product/ links — keep full domain

last_feedback: 2026-03-15 Dan email: Anti-force micro-phrases are canonical. Never make beginners feel wrong or inadequate. Use verbatim: 'Soften the inhale until the breath becomes quiet again.' 'In breathwork, steady usually goes further than intense.' /product-category/training-programs/ is 404 — never link.

2026-03-10 Dan email: Don't front-load or scatter heavy safety paragraphs — one brief consolidated safety note. Don't over-reference science for experiential/spiritual topics (pineal gland, third eye, energy breathing, Still Point). Dan is the final arbiter of Voice A posts.

2026-03-08 Paul Taubman (Digital Maestro, Dan agreed): Don't keyword-stuff headings — H1 establishes the keyword, vary all subsequent headings with synonyms and angles. Write for the reader, not the search engines.
off_limits: Claiming breathwork cures or treats medical conditions
Spiritual claims presented as established medical fact
